steps.title: The minimum weight decreased by 10 pounds
steps.content: The horse with the lowest reported weight actually weighs 10 pounds less than reported. This is the only change in the data.

steps.title: The median is position-based, not value-dependent
steps.content: With 15 horses, the median is the 8th value when the data is sorted in ascending order. The horse with the lowest weight is in position 1. When we decrease its weight by 10 pounds, it remains in position 1—it is still the lowest weight. The 8th position (where the median value sits) does not change, so the median itself does not change.

steps.title: Mean, range, and standard deviation all depend on values
steps.content:
• Mean depends on the sum of all weights. Decreasing one weight by 10 decreases the sum by 10, so the mean decreases.
  • Range = maximum − minimum. Decreasing the minimum by 10 increases the range by 10.
  • Standard deviation measures the spread of data around the mean. Both the mean and the spread change, so the standard deviation changes.

Only the median remains unchanged. That matches choice B — B is correct.
